title: Climbing at the Promontory
summary: Our first stop upon leaving shelter Cove was to hit the gym and take some showers at the planet fitness in Eureka California. While the gym itself was nice, the setting was not. It was located in the back of the shopping mall in a sketchy part of town that made us feel uncomfortable about leaving our van too too long. So, we opted for a quick shower and then headed further up the road towards Arcadia, California to do some laundry. While the laundry was going, I went for a great little run through the redwoods surrounding Humboldt State university. The trails were very smooth and the setting was beautiful and unlike what I am used to in the mountains and Desert. After our laundry was done, we met back up with Abby and played some disc golf in the redwoods. The course was very fun and it was miraculous that we didn't lose any of our discs as there were dents trees, and bushes every direction (we did, however, spend a very long time looking for discs caught in the bushes). That night we found a camping area a little bit north of town at a place called big Lagoon where we had a foggy evening, cooking dinner and relaxing in the van. the main objective the next day was to climb at the promontory, a Craig right on the Waters edge that is famous for a very hard rock climb. I've wanted to visit for many years so I was excited to finally make it there. despite the high tide, we were able to make it to the Climbing and did a few fun routes with waves bashing, just few feet away from us. However, as we moved towards the main wall at the Craig, we found that the rock quality deteriorated and were greeted with extremely Sandy and not that enjoyable crimes. We called it a day a little early and head into Crescent city to meet up again with Abby.

photos.caption: A disc golf tee box on top of a dead redwood!
A selfie from the top of a climb at the promontory.
